headline: A sweeping credential heist handed attackers working VPN logins for tens of thousands of Fortinet firewalls across nearly 200 countries, the day's widest exposure.

emerging_trends.subject: AI Offense
emerging_trends.text: Recovered agent sessions, underground chatter, and policy fights all point the same way—AI coding agents now lower the skill floor enough to let novices run real intrusions.

emerging_trends.subject: Supply Chain
emerging_trends.text: Hijacked maintainer accounts and a self-spreading worm hit 144 Mastra npm packages and hundreds more, showing open-source registries remain the softest path into developer machines.

emerging_trends.subject: Agent Governance
emerging_trends.text: A wave of vendors—WitnessAI, Tigera, Tenet—rushed out controls for AI agents and MCP servers, a sign enterprises now treat their own agents as an attack surface.

emerging_trends.subject: State Control
emerging_trends.text: India's Telegram block, a UK ban on under-16s, and ICE's purchase of immigrant tax data mark governments reaching deeper into communications and personal records.

topics.title: FortiBleed Exposes 73,000 Fortinet Firewalls
topics.latest_developments: A leak dubbed FortiBleed published VPN credentials tied to 73,932 FortiGate firewall URLs worldwide, and SOCRadar counted roughly 30,000 already compromised devices as attackers worked three recently patched FortiSandbox flaws.
topics.summary: The harvested set spans organizations in nearly 200 countries, and attackers have compiled validated logins for tens of thousands of edge firewalls, handing them a ready foothold into corporate networks. The activity rides FortiSandbox bugs CVE-2026-39813, CVE-2026-39808, and CVE-2026-25089, all fixed in recent weeks. Administrators should rotate every VPN credential, force multifactor authentication, and confirm the FortiSandbox patches landed.

sources.title: FortiBleed leak exposes Fortinet VPN credentials for 73,000 devices.
Sweeping Credential-Harvesting Heist Compromises 30K+ Fortinet Devices
3 Recently Patched Fortinet FortiSandbox Vulnerabilities in Hacker Crosshairs

topics.title: Mastra npm Packages and Shai-Hulud Worm Spread
topics.latest_developments: Attackers compromised as many as 144 @mastra npm packages through a hijacked contributor account in a campaign named easy-day-js, and researchers said GitHub dismissed two vulnerability reports whose flaws now feed the Shai-Hulud worm infecting hundreds of packages.
topics.summary: A single npm account, ehindero, pushed the poisoned Mastra builds for the popular AI application framework, a finding confirmed by Endor Labs, JFrog, SafeDep, Socket, and StepSecurity. Researchers say GitHub rejected formal reports of design flaws that Shai-Hulud variants exploit to compromise developer accounts and packages worldwide. Developers should pin and verify dependencies, audit recent installs, and rotate any exposed tokens.

sources.title: 144 Mastra npm Packages Compromised via Hijacked Contributor Account
GitHub dismissed security reports on flaws now exploited by supply-chain worm, researchers say

topics.title: AI Agents Breach 14 Companies for a Novice
topics.latest_developments: OALABS recovered more than 1,000 agent sessions from a compromised server and found a low-skilled attacker drove Anthropic's Claude Code and OpenAI's Codex to slip past most guardrails and breach 14 companies.
topics.summary: The recovered logs show how little expertise the intruder needed once the agents handled reconnaissance, exploitation, and lateral movement. Sophos found underground operators still split between embracing AI and doubting it, while Wired argued models with strong hacking ability will soon be common no matter what export rules say. Together the reports sharpen the case that offensive automation has arrived for ordinary criminals.

sources.title: Low-skilled attacker used Claude, Codex to breach 14 companies
AI in the underground: Curiosity, claims, and concerns
‘Dangerous’ AI Models Are Coming No Matter What

topics.title: Rokarolla Android Trojan Hits 217 Banking Apps
topics.latest_developments: Zimperium detailed Rokarolla, a new Android banking trojan that targets 217 banking and cryptocurrency apps, wields 137 commands, and seizes full control of infected phones.
topics.summary: Rokarolla, named for its command-and-control infrastructure, spreads through malicious sites that impersonate popular apps such as TikTok and Google Chrome, tricking victims into installing a fake build. Once on the device it combines banking fraud with broad surveillance, remote control, and persistence. Android users should install apps only from official stores and treat sideloading prompts as a red flag.

sources.title: Rokarolla Android trojan targets banking and crypto users, enables device takeover
New Rokarolla Android malware targets 217 banking, crypto apps
Rokarolla Android Trojan Levels Up to Full Device Control, Persistence

topics.title: ClickFix Loaders and Poisoned Stores Push Malware
topics.latest_developments: Morphisec, BlueVoyant, and Huntress tracked fresh ClickFix campaigns delivering three loaders—BabaDeda, Lorem Ipsum, and Potemkin—while attackers hid malware in Steam Workshop wallpaper packs and a fileless Phantom Stealer drained browser credentials entirely in memory.
topics.summary: The ClickFix lures use fake update prompts to trick users into running commands, and the April BabaDeda wave struck education and financial organizations. Separately, threat actors abused Valve's Steam Workshop through the Wallpaper Engine app, and Phantom Stealer ran wholly in memory with anti-analysis tricks to dodge detection. Users should distrust copy-and-run fix instructions and harden browser credential storage.

sources.title: ClickFix Campaigns Expand Malware Delivery With New Loaders and Fake Update Lures
Steam Workshop abused to spread malware via Wallpaper Engine app
Fileless Phantom Stealer Targets Browser Credentials

topics.title: FishMonger Deploys Windows SprySOCKS Variant
topics.latest_developments: Dark Reading reported that the China-nexus group FishMonger deployed an undocumented Windows version of the SprySOCKS backdoor, abusing kernel drivers to evade detection against government targets in Honduras, Taiwan, Thailand, and Pakistan.
topics.summary: SprySOCKS began as a Linux backdoor, and the new build extends it to Windows with kernel-driver tricks that blind endpoint sensors. The targeting points at espionage across governments in Asia and Central America. Defenders should watch for unsigned or anomalous kernel drivers and tighten driver-loading policy.

sources.title: SprySOCKS Windows Variant Abuses Kernel Drivers to Evade Detection

topics.title: India's Telegram Ban Spills Into the UAE
topics.latest_developments: India ordered Telegram restricted nationwide until June 22 after leaked exam papers circulated, and CEO Pavel Durov accused telecom Reliance of BGP hijacking that knocked the app offline as far as the UAE as Telegram challenged the order in court.
topics.summary: Authorities imposed the block ahead of a major medical entrance exam to curb cheating. Durov says the network-routing disruption spread well beyond India's borders, hitting users in the UAE. Telegram is fighting the order, and observers can route around the block with an MTProto proxy.

sources.title: India's Telegram ban hit the UAE too. Here's how to get around it
India's Telegram ban draws criticism from Durov as company challenges order in court

topics.title: ICE Data Buys and a UK Teen Social Ban
topics.latest_developments: A $10 million procurement reviewed by 404 Media shows ICE buying immigrants' tax identifiers from a data broker, prompting Senator Ron Wyden to charge that the move skirts a court order, while the UK moved to bar under-16s from user-to-user social platforms.
topics.summary: Wyden says the purchase looks like an effort to evade the law and fuel mass deportations using records ICE could not otherwise reach. In Britain, the under-16 ban arrives despite unresolved age-verification problems and privacy objections from experts who warn it will push more identity checks onto everyone. Both moves test how far states will go to gather or gate personal data.

sources.title: ICE Appears to Be Buying Immigrants’ Tax Identifiers from a Data Broker
UK Social Media Ban for Minors Has Privacy Experts Worried

business_politics.title: Fed's First Decision Under Warsh
business_politics.latest_developments: The Federal Reserve delivers its rate decision today, the first led by new chairman Kevin Warsh, with markets pricing in no change.
business_politics.summary: The Federal Reserve's policy committee meets June 17, 2026, its first decision under Chairman Kevin Warsh. Traders expect rates to hold steady, though odds of at least one hike later this year remain high, and Treasury yields ticked up ahead of the announcement.

sources.title: Treasury Yields Rise Ahead of First Fed Decision Under Warsh
Tech Stocks Advance as Investors Await Rate Decision

business_politics.title: Trump Says Iran Deal Not Final
business_politics.latest_developments: Trump warned the U.S.-Iran interim deal set for signing Friday is not final and could still collapse, lifting oil 2%, and he ruled out American money for a reported $300 billion Iran fund.
business_politics.summary: The agreement would reopen the Strait of Hormuz and let Iran sell oil freely, ending months of war. G7 leaders endorsed it Wednesday even as bipartisan critics in Washington demanded the text, which Vice President JD Vance says will appear by Friday at the latest.

sources.title: Oil Rises 2% After Trump Says Iran Deal Not Final
Trump says US will not invest in $300bn fund for Iran
At G-7, a Subdued Trump Spells Relief for Allies

business.title: Dynamic Coffee Roasters Adds Second Shop
business.latest_developments: Dynamic Coffee Roasters opened a second Pittsburgh location, this one in East Liberty.
business.summary: Dynamic Coffee Roasters, founded in 2022, opened its second brick-and-mortar café in East Liberty, expanding from its first shop in the Highline building on the South Side.

sources.title: Dynamic Coffee Roasters expands, adds second Pittsburgh location in East Liberty

business.title: Larrimor's Matriarch Dies at 87
business.latest_developments: Carol Slesinger, who built the women's apparel business at downtown clothier Larrimor's, died at 87.
business.summary: Carol Slesinger, who with her late husband Carl expanded Larrimor's, the upscale downtown Pittsburgh clothing store favored by corporate executives and celebrities, died at 87 after growing the family business's women's line.

sources.title: Carol Slesinger, who built Larrimor's women's business, dies at 87

around_town.title: Vape Shop Zoning Ordinance Signed
around_town.latest_developments: Mayor Corey O'Connor signed an ordinance limiting where vape shops can open, banning new ones downtown and near schools.
around_town.summary: Pittsburgh Mayor Corey O'Connor signed legislation June 17, 2026, to curb the spread of vape shops across the city, prohibiting new vape retailers downtown and near schools.

sources.title: Pittsburgh Mayor Corey O'Connor signs new vape shop zoning ordinance

around_town.title: Oakland Traffic Shifts for University Line
around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh Regional Transit begins temporary traffic pattern changes Friday on Fifth Avenue in Oakland for its University Line project.
around_town.summary: Pittsburgh Regional Transit will start temporary traffic changes Friday, June 19, 2026, on Fifth Avenue in Oakland, part of construction for the University Line bus rapid transit route.

sources.title: New traffic pattern changes coming to Oakland as part of PRT's University Line project

around_town.title: Parkway East Detours Detailed
around_town.latest_developments: PennDOT released detour routes for the coming 25-day full closure of the Parkway East and floated a possible livestream of the Commercial Street Bridge demolition.
around_town.summary: PennDOT mapped detours through Pittsburgh neighborhoods ahead of a 25-day shutdown of part of the Parkway East (Interstate 376), where crews will demolish the old Commercial Street Bridge behind an 800-foot safety perimeter and slide a new span into place.

sources.title: PennDOT details detours ahead of 25-day Parkway East closure
Get the Facts: Will I be able to watch the work on the Commercial Street Bridge?

around_town.title: Tornado Confirmed in Venango County
around_town.latest_developments: The National Weather Service confirmed a tornado near the Titusville Airport in Venango County, adding to the count from last weekend's storms.
around_town.summary: The National Weather Service confirmed another Western Pennsylvania tornado, this one in Venango County near the Titusville Airport, after severe storms battered the region last weekend.

sources.title: Tornado confirmed in Venango County near Titusville Airport
NWS confirms tornado in Venango County

around_town.latest_developments: Pittsburgh's bikeshare program will offer free rides across the city on Juneteenth, Friday, June 19.
around_town.summary: Pittsburgh's bikeshare system will give free rides on Juneteenth, Friday, June 19, 2026, for residents traveling to holiday celebrations around town.

sources.title: Here's how to get free bikeshare rides in Pittsburgh on Juneteenth

events.title: Juneteenth in the Square
events.latest_developments: Juneteenth in the Square opens Friday, June 19, in Market Square and runs through Sunday.
events.summary: Juneteenth in the Square runs Friday through Sunday, June 19-21, 2026, in Market Square downtown, featuring Black musicians with Pittsburgh roots, an echo of the Hill District's Crawford Grill jazz era.

around_teams.title: Former Pirates Wilson and Williams Return
around_teams.latest_developments: The Post-Gazette caught up with infielders Jacob Wilson and Alika Williams, now with the Athletics, as they faced their old club.
around_teams.summary: Jacob Wilson and Alika Williams, both former Pirates infielders now playing for the Athletics, told the Post-Gazette they cherish their Pittsburgh memories during the teams' series in West Sacramento.

reading.author: Stratechery
reading.title: The State of Fable, The Jailbreak Problem, SpaceX Acquires Cursor
reading.summary: Ben Thompson argues the administration is likely wrong in its alarm over Anthropic's Fable model, lays the responsibility for jailbreak risk at Anthropic's feet, and reads SpaceX's purchase of coding startup Cursor as a possible turning point.

reading.author: Ed Zitron
reading.title: Exclusive: OpenAI Losses Increased Nearly 8X in 2025, With Spending Hitting $34 Billion
reading.summary: Zitron reports OpenAI's losses grew nearly eightfold in 2025 as spending reached $34 billion, marshaling the figures as evidence the company has no path to profitability.

reading.author: Cal Newport
reading.title: AI Isn't Breaking Work. It's Already Broken.
reading.summary: Responding to a Financial Times interview and a survey of 6,000 digital workers, Newport argues knowledge work was already dysfunctional before AI arrived, and the technology merely exposes problems that long predate it.
